log.why: Some spells require a later upkeep action after initial casting.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Sustain Requirement Timing. Purpose: Sustain Requirement Timing exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about action-based upkeep. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Sustain Requirement Timing`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sustainRequirement.timing`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no sustain requirement.

acceptedValueNotes.later_turns: Use when the upkeep action applies on turns after the initial casting turn.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when canonical prose has no action-based sustain requirement.

log.why: The runtime needs to know who must pay an upkeep cost.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Sustain Requirement Actor. Purpose: Sustain Requirement Actor exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about who maintains a spell. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Sustain Requirement Actor`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sustainRequirement.actor`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no sustain requirement.

acceptedValueNotes.caster: Use when the spellcaster must spend the sustain action.
acceptedValueNotes.target: Use when the original spell target must spend the sustain action.
acceptedValueNotes.affected_creature: Use when any currently affected creature may be the sustain actor.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no sustain actor applies.

log.why: Canonical action category must not be flattened into action cost alone.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Sustain Requirement Action Type. Purpose: Sustain Requirement Action Type exists so runtime JSON preserves canonical wording such as Magic action. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Sustain Requirement Action Type`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sustainRequirement.actionKind`. Use `not_applicable` only when no sustain action category applies.

acceptedValueNotes.magic_action: Use when canonical prose specifically requires the Magic action.
acceptedValueNotes.standard_action: Use when canonical prose requires an ordinary action without the Magic-action tag.
acceptedValueNotes.bonus_action: Use when canonical prose requires a Bonus Action.
acceptedValueNotes.reaction: Use when canonical prose requires a Reaction.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no sustain action category applies.

log.why: Action-budget checks need the concrete cost separate from canonical action category.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Sustain Requirement Action Cost. Purpose: Sustain Requirement Action Cost exists so action-economy validation can read the upkeep cost directly. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Sustain Requirement Action Cost`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sustainRequirement.actionCost`. Use `not_applicable` only when no action-economy sustain cost applies.

acceptedValueNotes.action: Use when the sustain requirement consumes the actor's action.
acceptedValueNotes.bonus_action: Use when the sustain requirement consumes the actor's Bonus Action.
acceptedValueNotes.reaction: Use when the sustain requirement consumes the actor's Reaction.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no action-economy sustain cost applies.

log.why: The runtime needs the consequence when the required upkeep action is not paid.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Sustain Requirement Failure Outcome. Purpose: Sustain Requirement Failure Outcome exists so upkeep failure does not remain hidden in description text. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Sustain Requirement Failure Outcome`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sustainRequirement.failureOutcome`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no sustain requirement.

acceptedValueNotes.spell_ends: Use when failure to pay the upkeep action ends the whole spell.
acceptedValueNotes.effect_ends: Use when failure to pay the upkeep action ends only one effect packet.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no sustain failure consequence applies.
